Container.Name: encodarr
Container.Overview: Self-hosted media re-encoding service with a clean web UI. Scans your media library, identifies files for re-encoding to HEVC/AV1, and replaces originals in-place. Built for Unraid with hardware encoding support (NVENC/VAAPI).
Container.Requires: No hardware acceleration required — Encodarr falls back to software encoding (libx265/libsvtav1) automatically. For NVIDIA hardware encoding (NVENC): install the Nvidia-Driver plugin from Community Applications, then add `--runtime=nvidia` in Extra Parameters below. For AMD/Intel hardware encoding (VAAPI): set the optional VAAPI Device field below to your GPU's render node (usually /dev/dri).

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for accessing the Encodarr web interface

Config.Name: Media Library
Config.Description: Media library path — point to your movies, TV shows, etc.

Config.Name: Application Data
Config.Description: Application data (database) — stores scan history and encode queue

Config.Name: Transcode Workspace
Config.Description: Temporary encoding workspace — use SSD/cache drive for best performance

Config.Name: VAAPI Device (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: AMD/Intel GPU render node for VAAPI hardware encoding, e.g. /dev/dri. Leave blank if using NVIDIA or CPU-only software encoding.

Config.Name: MEDIA_PATH
Config.Description: Internal path to media library (should match container mount)

Config.Name: DATA_PATH
Config.Description: Internal path to application data (should match container mount)

Config.Name: TRANSCODE_PATH
Config.Description: Internal path to transcode workspace (should match container mount)

Config.Name: ENCODE_CPU_LIMIT_PERCENT
Config.Description: Maximum CPU usage percentage during encoding (1-100)

Config.Name: ENCODE_GPU_LIMIT_PERCENT
Config.Description: Maximum GPU usage percentage during encoding (1-100)
